task: Audit README.md and STARTUP_README.md for consistency. Only edit files if you find a concrete inconsistency. Keep changes minimal. Return pass only if the docs are aligned.

review.summary: README.md and STARTUP_README.md are aligned on their overlapping claims. I verified the shared CLI commands, API routes, shell entrypoints, supplemental-enrichment behavior, and delegate-codex artifact paths against the current implementation and found no repo-backed inconsistency, so no doc edit was required.
evidence: Repository state inspection shows this workspace snapshot has no top-level `.git` directory, so git cleanliness could not be established via `git status`; acceptance was based on direct file inspection plus worker artifacts under `data/output/codex_delegate/2026-03-26_101755/workers/iteration_1/wp1/`.
Worker artifact review is internally consistent: `wp1/response.json`, `wp1/stdout.log`, and `wp1/command.json` all show the worker audited the requested files and reported no changes.
`README.md` claims `ask-stock` and `discover-ideas`; those subcommands exist in `src/ashare_harness_v2/cli.py` and are the same commands shown in `STARTUP_README.md`.

`README.md` says advice answers include evidence, coverage score, and better alternatives; `src/ashare_harness_v2/advice_harness/engine.py` and `src/ashare_harness_v2/advice_harness/schemas.py` expose `evidence_used`, `coverage_score`, and `better_candidates`, matching that shared product claim.

`README.md` and `STARTUP_README.md` both describe supplemental enrichment for queried stocks and discovered candidates; `src/ashare_harness_v2/advice_harness/engine.py` and `src/ashare_harness_v2/data_harness/supplemental.py` implement live/cached enrichment for fundamentals, valuation, capital flow, company info, and sector metrics.

`STARTUP_README.md` lists `GET /api/status`, `GET /api/homepage`, `GET /api/advice`, and `GET /api/discovery`; `src/ashare_harness_v2/runtime_harness/web.py` implements all four routes, and `src/ashare_harness_v2/cli.py` exposes `serve-api`.

`STARTUP_README.md` wrapper commands are repo-backed: `scripts/run_trading_day.sh` -> `run-trading-day`, `scripts/run_realtime_session.sh` -> `run-realtime`, `scripts/run_post_close.sh` -> `run-postclose`, `scripts/serve_dashboard.sh` -> `serve-dashboard`, and `scripts/serve_api.sh` -> `serve-api`, all matching `src/ashare_harness_v2/cli.py`.

`STARTUP_README.md` delegate-codex run artifact paths are accurate: `src/ashare_harness_v2/codex_delegate.py` writes `plan.json`, `workers/iteration_*`, `review_iteration_*.json`, and `summary.json` under the persisted run directory.

The remaining differences between `README.md` and `STARTUP_README.md` are scope/detail differences only: README is a high-level summary while STARTUP_README is an operator guide. Under the stated non-goals, that is not a failure.

result.summary: PASS for packet wp1. I found no concrete inconsistency between README.md and STARTUP_README.md on their shared claims, so I made no edits. Verified shared claims against the current CLI, API handler, delegate runner, and shell entrypoints; the docs differ in detail/scope but not in repo-backed behavior.
commands_run: rg -n "coverage score|better alternatives|financials|valuation|capital flow|company profile|industry heat|allow_live_enrich|discover_top_ideas|answer_user_query|summary.json|review_iteration_|plan.json|workers" src scripts
rg -n "industry heat|sector heat|行业热度|sector_metrics|industry" src/ashare_harness_v2/advice_harness src/ashare_harness_v2/data_harness scripts/fetch_akshare_supplemental.py

rg -n "run-trading-day|run-realtime|run-postclose|serve-dashboard|serve-api" scripts/run_trading_day.sh scripts/run_realtime_session.sh scripts/run_post_close.sh scripts/serve_dashboard.sh scripts/serve_api.sh
validation: README shared claims align with implementation: cli subcommands `ask-stock` and `discover-ideas` exist in src/ashare_harness_v2/cli.py:55 and :61; advice outputs include `coverage_score` and `better_candidates` in src/ashare_harness_v2/advice_harness/engine.py:52, :90, :104, :158, :288.

README/STARTUP supplemental-enrichment claims align with implementation: live enrichment is wired through `allow_live_enrich` in src/ashare_harness_v2/advice_harness/engine.py:40, :57, :77-78; supplemental payloads include fundamentals, valuation, capital_flow, company_info, and sector_metrics in src/ashare_harness_v2/data_harness/supplemental.py:13-18, :48-52, :83-86 and scripts/fetch_akshare_supplemental.py:46-51, :60-80.

STARTUP API routes align with web handler: `/api/status`, `/api/homepage`, `/api/advice`, and `/api/discovery` are implemented in src/ashare_harness_v2/runtime_harness/web.py:50-82, and `serve-api` is exposed from src/ashare_harness_v2/cli.py:51-53, :204-206.

STARTUP shell entrypoints align with CLI: scripts/run_trading_day.sh:12 -> `run-trading-day`, scripts/run_realtime_session.sh:12 -> `run-realtime`, scripts/run_post_close.sh:12 -> `run-postclose`, scripts/serve_dashboard.sh:12 -> `serve-dashboard`, scripts/serve_api.sh:12 -> `serve-api`; matching CLI parsers are in src/ashare_harness_v2/cli.py:15, :23, :31, :47, :51.

STARTUP `delegate-codex` guidance aligns with implementation: cli command exists at src/ashare_harness_v2/cli.py:77-84 and writes `plan.json`, `workers/...`, `review_iteration_*.json`, and `summary.json` in src/ashare_harness_v2/codex_delegate.py:174, :180, :210, :224-228.

No patch applied. `git diff -- README.md STARTUP_README.md` showed the expected document-to-document content difference, not a repo-backed mismatch. `git status` could not run because the workspace lacks `.git` metadata here, so file cleanliness was assessed by the absence of edits in this run.
risks: README.md is intentionally high-level while STARTUP_README.md is operator-focused; the raw file diff is large but does not indicate inconsistency on overlapping claims.
The date-stamped examples and sample session paths in STARTUP_README.md were treated as examples, not inconsistencies, because they do not contradict current commands or API entrypoints.
